sonnet_review.biasedText: Donations began pouring into Black Lives Matter and affiliated racial justice organizations in 2020 following the murder of George Floyd, setting off a spending spree among leaders who suddenly had access to millions of dollars with very little oversight.
sonnet_review.reason: This exact phrasing appears in the article and uses evaluative language (“spending spree,” “very little oversight”) in the author’s voice. While I did not flag it initially, it is a defensible claim of slanted framing.
The term is present and carries a judgmental tone compared with a neutral alternative like “personal expenses.” It is a reasonable word-choice bias observation.

sonnet_review.biasedText: According to the group's 2020-2022 tax filings, only one third of the organization's $90 million in revenue was used to fund the charity.
sonnet_review.reason: This sentence is in the article; the word “only” implies a negative judgment without offering comparative context. It’s a reasonable, defensible bias detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: Donations began pouring into Black Lives Matter and affiliated racial justice organizations in 2020 following the murder of George Floyd, setting off a spending spree among leaders who suddenly had access to millions of dollars with very little oversight.
gpt_review.reason: The language is present and broadly negative (“spending spree… very little oversight”), generalizing beyond the specific case. It’s a defensible interpretation of negativity bias.

gpt_review.biasedText: setting off a spending spree among leaders who suddenly had access to millions of dollars with very little oversight
gpt_review.reason: This phrase is in the article and is sensational in tone. While somewhat overlapping with the first detection, it reasonably identifies loaded framing as spin.

gpt_review.biasedText: The article does not present any perspective or statement from Dickerson, Black Lives Matter OKC, the national BLM organization, or any supporters who might defend or contextualize the alleged actions.
gpt_review.reason: The piece provides allegations and critical context but no response or counterpoint from the accused or affiliated groups. While not obligatory in every report, noting this omission is a defensible bias observation.
